tagline: Thai funk cassettes, Houston highways, and the sonic threads connecting Houston's globe-trotting trio to a half-century of psychedelic soul.

nodes.name: Thai Funk & Luk Thung
nodes.connection: Guitarist Mark Speer and bassist Laura Lee Ochoa discovered Thai psych-funk via the Monrakplengthai blog. The reverb-drenched guitar, pentatonic melody, and hushed vocals became the spine of The Universe Smiles Upon You (2015).
nodes.videoTitle: Thai-Dreams (70s/80s Luk Thung, Luk Krung, Mor Lam) Live Vinyl Mix

nodes.name: Googoosh (Iranian Pop)
nodes.connection: Con Todo El Mundo (2018) drew from 1970s Iranian pop. Melismatic phrasing, modal scales, lush strings. Pitchfork: 'Iran is the obvious touchstone in Maria Tambien.'
nodes.videoTitle: Googoosh Full Concert at Hollywood Bowl

nodes.name: Mahalia Jackson (Gospel)
nodes.connection: Mark Speer and Donald 'DJ' Johnson played together in a Houston gospel church band before Khruangbin. The Guardian called their 2024 live show 'faintly liturgical.' The call-and-response and meditative pacing trace directly to this tradition.
nodes.videoTitle: Mahalia Jackson - How I Got Over (LIVE)

nodes.name: Leon Bridges
nodes.connection: Two collaborative EPs (Texas Sun, 2020; Texas Moon, 2022) revealed the deep Black Texas musical heritage underpinning Khruangbin's instrumental language.
nodes.videoTitle: Leon Bridges: NPR Music Tiny Desk Concert

nodes.name: Kool & the Gang
nodes.connection: Khruangbin played Summer Madness in live medleys for years before recording it for Late Night Tales (2020). Pitchfork: 'Onstage, Khruangbin have long played a medley weaving together old funk and hip-hop rhythms, slotting Summer Madness alongside Nothin but a G Thang.'
nodes.videoTitle: Summer Madness Live - Kool & the Gang

nodes.name: Vieux Farka Toure
nodes.connection: Collaboration album Ali (2022) blended West African desert blues with Khruangbin's reverb-glazed groove, making explicit the structural relationship between Malian music and Texas blues.
nodes.videoTitle: Vieux Farka Toure - Full Performance (Live on KEXP)

nodes.name: Jimi Hendrix
nodes.connection: Mark Speer's guitar technique, reverb-heavy sustained phrases, upper-register melody, mood-generating atmosphere, draws on late-1960s psych-rock. The Guardian: 'reverb-laden twang of spaghetti western melodies.'

nodes.name: Bonobo
nodes.connection: Khruangbin formed while opening for Bonobo's 2010 US tour. Bonobo included 'A Calf Born in Winter' on his 2013 Late Night Tales mix, introducing Khruangbin to a global audience. Pitchfork: 'It was on Bonobo's own 2013 mix that much of the world was introduced to Khruangbin.'
nodes.videoTitle: Bonobo Live At Royal Albert Hall

nodes.name: The Meters
nodes.connection: Laura Lee McBride grew up in Houston's R&B and gospel scene, where The Meters were foundational listening. Their locked-in, minimal pocket, one riff repeated until hypnotic, is the structural engine beneath every Khruangbin groove.

nodes.name: Serge Gainsbourg
nodes.connection: Mark Speer has discussed DJing Gainsbourg records at after-parties on European tours. Pitchfork: 'Mark Speer might fold in cuts like Serge Gainsbourg sleaze in Paris or funky molam in Bangkok.'
nodes.videoTitle: Serge Gainsbourg - Bonnie And Clyde (Live au Zenith de Paris 1988)

nodes.name: Thievery Corporation
nodes.connection: Pitchfork placed Khruangbin in a lineage with Thievery Corporation as practitioners of 'dub-jammy ethereal instrumental music that synthesizes global traditions without exoticizing the source material.'
nodes.videoTitle: Thievery Corporation - Full Performance (Live on KEXP)
